content: Neuromodulation uses electrical or magnetic stimulation to alter neural activity for therapeutic purposes. Deep brain stimulation (DBS): surgically implanted electrodes deliver high-frequency stimulation to specific brain targets. FDA-approved indications: Parkinson's disease (subthalamic nucleus or GPi targets—improves motor fluctuations, dyskinesias, tremor), essential tremor (VIM thalamus—reduces tremor), dystonia (GPi—especially generalized/segmental dystonia), OCD (anterior limb of internal capsule—refractory cases). Investigational: depression (subcallosal cingulate, VC/VS), epilepsy (ANT thalamus—approved for focal epilepsy), Tourette syndrome, addiction. Mechanism: high-frequency stimulation inhibits pathological neuronal firing, modulates network activity. Transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS): non-invasive magnetic coil over scalp induces electrical currents in cortex. FDA-approved: major depressive disorder (left DLPFC—rTMS; bilateral—theta burst), OCD (medial prefrontal/anterior cingulate), smoking cessation, migraine with aura (single-pulse TMS). Vagus nerve stimulation (VNS): implanted pulse generator stimulates left vagus nerve—approved for refractory epilepsy and treatment-resistant depression.
